calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jul-25-2016 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-22-2016 Continued To Aug-22-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-09-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. MICHAEL G. THOMAS, ESQ. of Kern, Noda, Devine & Segal, Tel No. 415-474-1900 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. PAUL YEE, ESQ. of Law Office of Lori R. Mayfield, Tel No. 510-457-9923 appearing for Defendants Mag Management, Inc. and 351 Turk Street Apartments TIC.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS MAG MANAGEMENT, INC. AND 351 TURK STREET APARTMENTS TIC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendants did not satisfy their initial burden of production. The complaint alleges that plaintiff fell four stories from an "unsecured and unreasonably dangerous window configuration with an extremely low window sill" and defendants failed to install safety features and comply with safety requirements regarding the window. However, defendants' separate statement of facts does not include a single fact refuting these allegations. In some situations an owner and manager of an apartment building has a duty of care to prevent persons from falling out of windows. (Amos v. Alpha Property Management (1999) 73 Cal.App.4th 895). Nor do the facts that are included in defendants' separate statement establish as a matter of law that defendants are relieved from liability by any "superseding intervening cause" as argued by defendants. Prevailing party shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the court which may be e-mailed to contestdept302tr@sftc.org. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
